LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED - SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE SOLE RECOURSE RULE DOES NOT BAR THIS ACTION. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. WHERE A CONTRACT IS AMBIGUOUS, "A GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT ADMITS NOT ONLY THE CONTENTS OF THE INSTRUMENT BUT ALSO ANY PLEADED MEANING TO WHICH THE INSTRUMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE." (ARAGON HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES INC. (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239). THE CONTRACT HERE IS AMBIGUOUS AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE INTERPRETATION. DEFENDANT TO ANSWER BY JANUARY 13, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mark J. Divelbiss, Esq. of the Law Offices of Mark Divelbiss, Tel. 510.339.1755, for defendant and moving party;
Brian D. Liddicoat, Esq. of the Law Offices of Brian D. Liddicoat, Tel. 831.594.4418, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Regarding the first, second, third and fourth causes of action, plaintiff has raised triable issues regarding whether any funds contributed by plaintiff to the partnership that are alleged in the complaint were contributed by plaintiff pursuant to section 2.5(iii) of the Agreement. (Nawrocki Decl. 2, 3, 5-6.) Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue as to whether the loan giving rise to the Deed of Trust was a personal loan to Defendant that Defendant was obligated to repay. (Nawrocki Decl. 3.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Mark J. Divelbiss (510.339.1755) for defendant and moving party; Brian D. Liddicoat (831.594.4418) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled. Where a contract is ambiguous, "a general demurrer to the complaint admits not only the contents of the instrument but also any pleaded meaning to which the instrument is reasonably susceptible." (Aragon Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239). The contract here is ambiguous and plaintiff has alleged a reasonably susceptible interpretation. Defendant to answer by January 13, 2012. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

Plaintiff must plead facts showing the sole recourse rule does not bar this action. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order.
